content.text: When a Claude initiative grows beyond a single team, coordination overhead becomes a program risk in its own right. Agile-at-scale frameworks — SAFe, LeSS, and Scrum@Scale — provide different answers to the question of how multiple teams working on related Claude capabilities should synchronise, plan, and deliver together. Choosing the right framework depends on team count, whether teams share a product vision, regulatory requirements, and how much coordination overhead the organisation can sustain.

attrs.alt: Side-by-side comparison of SAFe, LeSS, and Scrum@Scale frameworks showing structure layers, Claude program fit, and overhead characteristics

content.text: Feature teams pulling from one backlog
Scrum of Scrums + EAT for org impediments
Selecting a Framework for Your Claude Program
The framework choice should follow the program's structural constraints rather than the organisation's existing preferences:

from dataclasses import dataclass
from enum import Enum
from typing import Optional

class AgileFramework(Enum):
    SAFE          = "safe"
    LESS          = "less"
    SCRUM_AT_SCALE = "scrum_at_scale"

@dataclass
class ProgramContext:
    team_count: int
    has_shared_product_vision: bool       # all teams building toward one product?
    regulatory_compliance_required: bool  # finance / healthcare / gov?
    existing_scrum_adoption: bool         # teams already run Scrum?
    quarterly_planning_cadence: bool      # org already does quarterly cycles?
    max_coordination_overhead: str        # "low" / "medium" / "high"

def recommend_framework(ctx: ProgramContext) -> AgileFramework:
    """Select the most appropriate agile-at-scale framework for a Claude program."""
    if ctx.team_count >= 20 or ctx.regulatory_compliance_required:
        return AgileFramework.SAFE
    if ctx.has_shared_product_vision and ctx.team_count <= 8:
        return AgileFramework.LESS
    if ctx.existing_scrum_adoption and ctx.team_count <= 15:
        return AgileFramework.SCRUM_AT_SCALE
    # Default for mid-size programs without strong constraints
    return AgileFramework.SCRUM_AT_SCALE

# Example: mid-size Claude enterprise with existing Scrum teams
ctx = ProgramContext(
    team_count=6,
    has_shared_product_vision=False,  # finance / hr / sales all separate
    regulatory_compliance_required=False,
    existing_scrum_adoption=True,
    quarterly_planning_cadence=True,
    max_coordination_overhead="medium",
)
print(recommend_framework(ctx))  # → AgileFramework.SCRUM_AT_SCALE

Program Increment Planning is SAFe's signature event — a 2-day in-person or virtual gathering where all teams in the ART plan the next 10 weeks together. For Claude programs, PI Planning surfaces cross-team API dependencies, shared prompt library decisions, and model access constraints before teams start building:

import anthropic

def generate_pi_planning_agenda(
    program_name: str,
    teams: list[str],
    strategic_themes: list[str],
    known_risks: list[str],
    duration_days: int = 2,
) -> str:
    """Generate a PI Planning event agenda tailored for a Claude enterprise program."""
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()

    message = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=500,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"""
You are a Release Train Engineer (RTE) creating the agenda for a {duration_days}-day
PI Planning event for "{program_name}", an enterprise Claude AI program.

Teams participating: {", ".join(teams)}
Strategic themes for this PI: {"; ".join(strategic_themes)}
Known risks entering the PI: {"; ".join(known_risks)}

Generate a realistic day-by-day agenda with time slots. Include:
- Day 1: Business context, product vision, architecture vision, team breakouts, draft plans
- Day 2: Team plan reviews, risk identification (ROAM), PI Objectives finalisation, confidence vote
- Claude-specific items: shared prompt library review, model access planning, cross-team API dependency coordination

Format as a structured agenda with times and owners. ≤ 250 words.
"""}],
    )
    return message.content[0].text


# Example
agenda = generate_pi_planning_agenda(
    program_name="Claude Enterprise AI Program Q3",
    teams=["Finance AI", "HR AI", "Sales AI", "Platform", "Security"],
    strategic_themes=[
        "Reduce operational cost by 30% through Claude automation",
        "Achieve SOC-2 compliance for all Claude integrations",
        "Launch RAG-powered search across all business units",
    ],
    known_risks=[
        "RAG pipeline not yet security-approved",
        "Model access quota may limit parallel rollouts",
        "Finance team capacity at 60% due to quarter-end work",
    ],
)
Scrum of Scrums for Claude Teams

In Scrum@Scale, the Scrum of Scrums (SoS) is a weekly cross-team sync where an ambassador from each team reports on three questions: what did my team complete that affects other teams, what will we work on next that others should know about, and what is blocking us that another team can help with. Claude generates the async summary:

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import date
from typing import Optional

@dataclass
class TeamImpediment:
    team: str
    description: str
    blocking_other_teams: bool
    raised_at: date
    resolved_at: Optional[date] = None

@dataclass
class ScrumOfScrumsSession:
    date: date
    teams_present: list[str]
    cross_team_updates: list[str]
    impediments_raised: list[TeamImpediment]
    decisions_made: list[str]
    next_session_date: date

def generate_sos_summary(session: ScrumOfScrumsSession) -> str:
    """Use Claude to write a Scrum of Scrums session summary for async distribution."""
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()

    blocking = [i for i in session.impediments_raised if i.blocking_other_teams]
    imp_txt = "\n".join(
        f"- {i.team}: {i.description}" + (" ← CROSS-TEAM BLOCK" if i.blocking_other_teams else "")
        for i in session.impediments_raised
    )
    message = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=250,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": f"""
Write a concise Scrum of Scrums session summary for async distribution.

Date: {session.date}
Teams: {", ".join(session.teams_present)}
Cross-team updates: {"; ".join(session.cross_team_updates)}
Impediments: {imp_txt if imp_txt else "None raised"}
Decisions: {"; ".join(session.decisions_made)}
Next session: {session.next_session_date}

Format:
1. Session status (1 line)
2. Cross-team highlights (2–3 bullets)
3. Impediments needing action (with owner and ETA) — only cross-team ones
4. Decisions taken
Keep total under 120 words.
"""}],
    )
    return message.content[0].text
SAFe PI Objectives for Claude Initiatives
PI Objectives are the team-level commitments that come out of PI Planning. For Claude teams, objectives should be concrete and outcome-oriented, not tool-oriented:
Integrate Claude API into the contract review tool
Reduce contract review time from 47 to 20 minutes for legal team (40 contracts/day)
Build RAG pipeline for HR documents
Enable HR chatbot to answer 80% of tier-1 policy questions without human agent involvement
Sales AI Proposal Generator producing first draft in < 2 min; pilot with 5 account managers
Go-live for Finance AI with SOC-2 audit trail; all data stays in VPC
LeSS for a Unified Claude Platform

When all Claude initiatives across the enterprise feed into a single AI assistant platform, LeSS is the simplest option. One Product Backlog, one Product Owner (typically the Head of AI Platform), and multiple feature teams pulling work:

@dataclass
class LeSSProductBacklog:
    product_name: str
    product_owner: str
    items: list[dict]   # each item: {"id", "title", "type", "team_affinity", "points"}

    def items_for_sprint(self, sprint_capacity_points: int) -> list[dict]:
        """Return top backlog items up to sprint capacity (simple greedy selection)."""
        selected, total = [], 0
        for item in sorted(self.items, key=lambda i: i.get("priority", 999)):
            if total + item["points"] <= sprint_capacity_points:
                selected.append(item)
                total += item["points"]
        return selected

# Single backlog for a unified Claude AI Platform
backlog = LeSSProductBacklog(
    product_name="Enterprise Claude AI Platform",
    product_owner="Head of AI Platform",
    items=[
        {"id": "PBI-101", "title": "Claude API gateway with rate limiting per team", "type": "feature", "team_affinity": "platform", "points": 8, "priority": 1},
        {"id": "PBI-102", "title": "Shared prompt library with versioning", "type": "feature", "team_affinity": "all", "points": 5, "priority": 2},
        {"id": "PBI-103", "title": "HR policy chatbot RAG integration", "type": "feature", "team_affinity": "hr-ai", "points": 13, "priority": 3},
        {"id": "PBI-104", "title": "Finance AI contract review — clause extraction", "type": "feature", "team_affinity": "finance-ai", "points": 8, "priority": 4},
    ],
)

# Sprint planning: 3 teams × 20 points each = 60 points capacity
sprint_items = backlog.items_for_sprint(sprint_capacity_points=60)
print([i["title"] for i in sprint_items])
Claude programs have scaling challenges that traditional agile frameworks don't address directly. These require explicit additions to the standard framework:

: When 6 teams all hit the same Claude API endpoint, rate limits and quota allocation become an ART-level concern. The Platform team should be a shared service team in SAFe, or a 'component team' in LeSS, responsible for API gateway and quota management.

: Prompts developed by the Finance AI team often benefit the Legal AI team. A shared prompt library backlog item in LeSS, or a Solution Intent artifact in SAFe, captures and distributes prompt patterns across teams.

: Regular model evaluation (is Claude still performing accurately after prompt or model updates?) needs to be a recurring team task in the sprint, not a one-time activity. Add it as a recurring backlog item.

: Data accessed by one team's Claude integration may have governance implications for another team's RAG pipeline. A working group or system architect role (in SAFe) or sprint goal constraint (in LeSS) handles this.
Shared Model Access and Prompt Libraries at Scale
is the first friction point when multiple Agile Release Trains or LeSS Areas all call Claude simultaneously. Without governance, teams hit rate limits unpredictably, slowing their sprint ceremonies.
Place a lightweight gateway between teams and the Anthropic API. The gateway enforces per-team quotas, routes burst traffic across accounts, and logs usage for capacity planning.

# api_gateway.py — thin FastAPI wrapper used by all ART teams
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, Header
import anthropic, asyncio, time
from collections import defaultdict

app = FastAPI()
client = anthropic.Anthropic()

# Per-team token budgets (replenish hourly)
TEAM_BUDGET = 200_000          # input tokens per hour per team
usage: dict[str, int] = defaultdict(int)
reset_at: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)

def _check_budget(team: str, tokens: int) -> None:
    now = time.time()
    if now > reset_at[team]:
        usage[team] = 0
        reset_at[team] = now + 3600
    if usage[team] + tokens > TEAM_BUDGET:
        raise HTTPException(429, f"Team {team} hourly budget exhausted")
    usage[team] += tokens

@app.post("/complete")
async def complete(body: dict, x_team: str = Header(...)):
    prompt = body.get("prompt", "")
    estimated = len(prompt) // 4          # rough token estimate
    _check_budget(x_team, estimated)
    response = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
    )
    return {"text": response.content[0].text, "team": x_team}
reduce duplication. Store versioned system prompts in a git-backed registry; teams import them as dependencies, and a prompt review board approves changes before they reach production.

# prompt_registry.py — read-only client for the shared prompt store
import json, pathlib

REGISTRY = pathlib.Path("prompts/registry.json")   # versioned in git

def get_prompt(name: str, version: str = "latest") -> str:
    registry = json.loads(REGISTRY.read_text())
    versions = registry.get(name, {})
    if version == "latest":
        version = max(versions.keys())
    entry = versions.get(version)
    if not entry:
        raise KeyError(f"Prompt '{name}' v{version} not found")
    return entry["system_prompt"]

# Usage inside a Feature Team
system = get_prompt("sprint-review-summariser", version="1.2")
When many teams share Claude-powered tooling, individual teams may tune prompts that degrade quality for others. A structured evaluation cadence surfaces regressions before they reach users.
Reserve one sprint per Program Increment for an evaluation sprint focused entirely on measuring prompt quality rather than shipping features.

# eval_cadence.py — automated regression test runner
import anthropic, json
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class EvalCase:
    prompt: str
    expected_keywords: list[str]
    must_not_contain: list[str] = None

def run_regression(cases: list[EvalCase], system_prompt: str) -> dict:
    client = anthropic.Anthropic()
    results = []
    for case in cases:
        resp = client.messages.create(
            model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
            max_tokens=512,
            system=system_prompt,
            messages=[{"role": "user", "content": case.prompt}]
        )
        text = resp.content[0].text.lower()
        hits = sum(1 for kw in case.expected_keywords if kw.lower() in text)
        blocks = sum(1 for kw in (case.must_not_contain or []) if kw.lower() in text)
        results.append({
            "prompt": case.prompt[:60],
            "keyword_hit_rate": hits / max(len(case.expected_keywords), 1),
            "blocked_phrases": blocks,
            "pass": hits >= len(case.expected_keywords) * 0.8 and blocks == 0
        })
    passed = sum(1 for r in results if r["pass"])
    return {"passed": passed, "total": len(results), "details": results}

# Example usage
SPRINT_REVIEW_CASES = [
    EvalCase("Summarise: Team A completed auth service", ["auth", "completed"], ["failed"]),
    EvalCase("List blockers: none reported", ["no blockers", "none"], ["critical"]),
]
report = run_regression(SPRINT_REVIEW_CASES, get_prompt("sprint-review-summariser"))
print(json.dumps(report, indent=2))
Cross-Team Data Governance for AI Features

Scaling Claude use across an organisation introduces data governance obligations that single-team pilots never surface: PII in prompts, model training opt-outs, audit trails for regulated outputs, and cross-jurisdiction data residency.
: Strip or mask PII before any text leaves the team boundary. Run a lightweight classifier on every prompt at the gateway.
: Persist a hash of every prompt/response pair with team ID, timestamp, and model version. Enables incident investigation without storing raw content.
: Tag each API call with the data-classification label of the content (PUBLIC / INTERNAL / CONFIDENTIAL). Block CONFIDENTIAL calls from crossing regional API endpoints.
: Set the Anthropic API header to opt out of model training for all calls involving customer data. Document this in the architecture decision record.
: Route PUBLIC content to claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (fast/cheap); INTERNAL to claude-sonnet-5; CONFIDENTIAL to on-premises or private-cloud deployment if required.

# data_classifier.py — lightweight PII and classification scanner
import re

PII_PATTERNS = [
    (re.compile(r'\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b'), "SSN"),
    (re.compile(r'\b[A-Z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z]{2,}\b', re.I), "email"),
    (re.compile(r'\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?|5[1-5][0-9]{14})\b'), "credit_card"),
]

def classify_prompt(text: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]:
    """Return (classification, [detected_pii_types])."""
    found = [label for pattern, label in PII_PATTERNS if pattern.search(text)]
    if found:
        return "CONFIDENTIAL", found
    keywords = ["revenue", "salary", "employee id", "patient", "contract"]
    if any(kw in text.lower() for kw in keywords):
        return "INTERNAL", []
    return "PUBLIC", []

def sanitise(text: str) -> str:
    for pattern, label in PII_PATTERNS:
        text = pattern.sub(f"[{label}]", text)
    return text

# At gateway boundary:
classification, pii_types = classify_prompt(raw_prompt)
if classification == "CONFIDENTIAL":
    safe_prompt = sanitise(raw_prompt)
else:
    safe_prompt = raw_prompt
# Forward safe_prompt to Claude with appropriate model routing

SAFe, LeSS, and Scrum@Scale each provide a viable structure for scaling Claude programs beyond a single team. SAFe's PI Planning is particularly valuable for large, multi-team programs with compliance requirements — surfacing API dependencies and model access constraints before teams commit to sprint plans. LeSS is the simplest choice when all Claude work shares one product vision. Scrum@Scale's Scrum of Scrums fills the coordination gap for teams already running Scrum without adding the full weight of SAFe's portfolio layer. In all frameworks, Claude accelerates the coordination work itself — generating PI Planning agendas, SoS summaries, and PI Objective drafts from structured program data.
